title: phlomoides codonantha
intro: Phlomoides codonantha, also known as Tibetan knotweed, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Phlomoides codonantha are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or by extracting its essential oils, which can be used to treat a range of health problems.
